heading: Clearinghouse of newborn screening information
label: 300b–11 Clearinghouse of newborn screening information

children.text: enable parents and family members of newborns, health professionals, industry representatives, and other members of the public to increase their awareness, knowledge, and understanding of newborn screening;
increase awareness, knowledge, and understanding of newborn diseases and screening services for expectant individuals and families;

maintain current information on quality indicators to measure performance of newborn screening, such as false-positive rates and other quality indicators as determined by the Advisory Committee under ;section 300b–10 of this title
maintain current information on the number of conditions for which screening is conducted in each State; and
disseminate available evidence-based guidelines related to diagnosis, counseling, and treatment with respect to conditions detected by newborn screening.
is updated on a regular basis, but not less than quarterly; and

children.label: A
children.text: links to Government-sponsored, non-profit, and other Internet websites of laboratories that have demonstrated expertise in newborn screening that supply research-based information on newborn screening tests currently available throughout the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: information about newborn conditions and screening services available in each State from laboratories certified under subpart 2 of part F of subchapter II, including information about supplemental screening that is available but not required, in the State where the infant is born;

children.label: C
children.text: current research on both treatable and not-yet treatable conditions for which newborn screening tests are available;

children.label: D
children.text: the availability of Federal funding for newborn and child screening for heritable disorders including grants authorized under the Newborn Screening Saves Lives Reauthorization Act of 2014; and

children.label: E
children.text: other relevant information as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Nonduplication
children.label: c Nonduplication
children.text: In carrying out activities under this section, the Secretary shall ensure that such activities minimize duplication and supplement, not supplant, existing information sharing efforts.
